notes.note: Patrocinium: the protective relationship a Roman noble extended over a foreign community he had taken under his fides --- often inherited from a magistrate-ancestor who had founded the bond. Whole Greek cities had Roman patrons of this kind.

Humanitas: a key term of Cicero's ethical vocabulary --- the cultivated, generous bearing of a civilised man, expressed especially in mercy and forbearance toward the weak. To act inhumaniter was a serious charge.

Magnum theatrum: a Ciceronian image for the public stage that a foreign command offers --- a wider audience than Rome itself, and one before which a young noble's character is more visibly tested. Compare De Officiis 3.74.
Clientelae: the standing dependencies of foreign communities and individuals on a Roman patron and his line. To inherit them required confirmation; new services bound them anew.
